title: The Day That Comes Without Warning
subtitle: As in the days of Noah and of Lot — the sudden coming of the Lord

stillness: The day of the Lord is uncertain;
let it not find you unprepared.
gospel: As it was in the days of Noah,
so shall the coming of the Son of Man be.
For as in the days before the flood
until the day Noah entered the ark,
until the flood came and swept them all away;
so shall the coming of the Son of Man be.
Likewise, as it was in the days of Lot:
but on the day Lot went out of Sodom,
it rained fire and brimstone from heaven,
on the day the Son of Man is revealed.
behold: Behold Noah building the ark, year upon year,
preaching the flood that is to come.
Though he was silent with his voice, he spoke by his work;
though his tongue was still, by the building he cried out.
For he was doing a new thing, a thing never seen before,
warning all: against new perils, prepare new shelters.
He spoke by his labor, and said the Gospel word of the Lord:
If you will not believe me, believe my works.
But the perverse, seeing the ark built through so many years,
rejoiced without sorrow, foreseeing nothing of what was to come,
and despised the judgments of God,
not waking, but slumbering in their wickedness;
and suddenly they were swallowed by the flood.
Behold Lot going out from Sodom,
and the Lord raining down fire against their unbridled burning,
and brimstone against their stench, from heaven,
and the cities of the plain laid waste.
consider: Their destruction was sudden —
not because it had not been foretold,
but because no one believed it.
Consider, as Beda says: it is not the marriages or the food that are condemned,
but the immoderate use of things lawful.
Not because they did these things, but because, giving themselves wholly to them,
they despised the judgments of God — and so they perished by water.
And in the days of Lot the Lord passes over their great and unspeakable crime,
and names only the lighter faults —
that you may understand: if things lawful, things without which this life is not lived,
are punished by fire and brimstone when used immoderately,
with what penalty shall the unlawful be struck?
And Chrysostom: what wonder if men hear the world will perish, and do not believe?
Daily they hear that they themselves will die, as often as they see others dead,
and yet they do not believe that they will die.
For if they believed they would die,
they would not do the things for which they die a death deserved.
So many do not think upon their own death,
though they shall most certainly die;
and often death comes when no danger is feared.
And when they shall say, peace and safety,
as men secure, fearing nothing,
sudden destruction shall come upon them,
and they shall not escape.
prayer: grant me to go before the uncertainty of the day of your coming
by watching and by working well,
lest, given over to pleasures and to vanities,
I be found suddenly unprepared on that day.
Grant me then to be counted, by your grace, among your elect,
and with them to be mercifully taken up to joys eternal;
and so, with those same elect and your saints —
whose youth shall be renewed like the eagle's through the resurrection —
may I, by your mercy, where you are gathered in your glorified body,
be made worthy to be filled with the sight of you,
and, rejoicing, to praise you without ceasing, without end.

latinJewel.latin: Noe, etsi tacebat voce, opere loquebatur; si et lingua silebat, fabricatione clamabat.
latinJewel.english: Noah, though he was silent with his voice, spoke by his work; though his tongue was still, by the building he cried out.
